layman_headline: Extreme fear grips crypto markets — patience is the best play right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is deep in fear territory with a Fear & Greed score of just 12 out of 100, and it's getting worse. Bitcoin has dropped nearly 8% this week and is sitting below $60,000, dragging most altcoins down with it. While a few small coins have pumped recently (like TAC and VELVET), those moves look overextended and risky to chase. The safest approach right now is to wait for clearer signs of a bottom before committing capital.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines with cash ready. The market is in panic mode, which eventually creates great buying opportunities, but we haven't seen a clear reversal signal yet. Watch for Bitcoin to stabilize above $58,000 and for fear to stop worsening before entering any new trades.
expert_reasoning: The market environment is extremely hostile for new entries: Fear & Greed at 12 and worsening, BTC down ~8% on the week below $60K, and BTC dominance at 55.7% crushing altcoins. Among the candidates, TAC (+197% 7d) and VELVET (+279% 7d) are massively overextended with overbought RSIs near 70 — classic blow-off tops that could retrace 40-60%. M and KITE have strongly bearish chart structures with -2.0 bias scores and Three Black Crows patterns, making them falling knife traps despite oversold readings. SOL shows relative strength (flat on the week) but has terrible risk/reward at current levels with resistance only 0.8% away. GWEI is the best candidate with a Morning Star reversal, +1.40 chart bias, and RSI at 62, but it needs to pull back to the $0.14-0.15 range to offer proper risk/reward. No candidate meets our criteria for immediate entry with a clear stop-loss and favorable setup.
risk_factors: BTC breaking below $58,000 could trigger a broader market cascade and invalidate all alt setups
Worsening Fear & Greed trend (12 and declining) suggests capitulation selling may not be over yet
High BTC dominance at 55.7% means capital is rotating out of alts — fighting the trend on small caps is dangerous
watch_list: Bitcoin price action around $58,000 support — a break below could trigger another leg down
GWEI for a pullback to the $0.14-0.15 zone which would create a proper entry with stop at $0.125
Fear & Greed index for any stabilization or uptick — a move back above 20 would signal the worst may be over
market_context: Crypto markets are in deep Extreme Fear (12/100, worsening) with BTC down 8% on the week to $59,715. High BTC dominance at 55.7% is crushing altcoins, and most candidates are either overextended pumps or falling knives — neither offers a clean swing entry right now.
context_banner: Extreme Fear dominates with no clear reversal signals yet — capital preservation is the priority while waiting for a proper bottom to form.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 12 (Extreme Fear) and worsening from 18 yesterday — market is in panic but hasn't shown signs of bottoming yet.
BTC dominance at 55.7% signals capital flight from alts to Bitcoin, making altcoin swing trades higher risk.
TAC (RSI 68) and VELVET (RSI 70) are overbought after 197% and 279% weekly gains respectively — high correction risk makes them untouchable.
GWEI's Morning Star reversal pattern with +1.40 chart bias is the cleanest bullish setup, but the 40% weekly gain means chasing now offers poor risk/reward.
ETH derivatives reset noted by Blockworks suggests potential positioning shift, but broader macro headwinds from AI spending collapse fears weigh on risk assets.
